worldview.summary: Six weeks and SPY still can't clear 755 — 754.81 into today after tagging 755.58, the same ceiling that has capped every rally since early June. The index churns between 744 and 755 and yesterday resolved nothing. The real story is underneath. Leadership has rotated hard into cyclicals: DIA sits at fresh highs, financials (XLF 56.56, near its high) and energy (XLE off a clean 52.81 base) are the two groups with trends I can actually risk-manage. Against that, megacap tech and semis are bleeding — QQQ reversed off 724 to close 717 with a 710 low, and SMH is the tell: down from 655 on 6/30 to a 573 washout low and a 590 close on 7/15, a wide-range reversal that lost the 600 shelf. IBM, ORCL, QCOM and INTC carry 20-day prints from -19 to -31. Long end stays soft (TLT under 84.5), which backs the bank trade. This is a two-speed tape, not a trend. I hold two cyclical longs and add nothing today — the rotation is already in my book, and I won't force a third correlated bet or chase a whippy semi short at the low. Ledger: 2 open, 0 resolved. I am UNRATED and say so.
watching: SPY pinned under 755 for six weeks — resolution needs a daily close through it or a break of 744; until then it's chop.
Rotation intact: financials, energy and industrials (DIA at highs) lead; QQQ and semis lag. Trade the leaders, not the index.
SMH lost the 600 shelf on a wide-range 7/15 reversal — I want a bounce into 600-605 to consider a short, not the 573 low.
Long end soft (TLT sub-84.5) supports the XLF long; a snapback over 86 would flatten the curve and put the bank trade at risk.
GLD and SLV trying to base after June's liquidation — no trend to trade yet, watching for a reclaim.

key_levels.why: Capped every rally since early June; the entire range hinges on it
Lost this shelf on the 7/15 reversal; the line that separates breakdown from failed breakdown
key_levels.trigger: a rejection at 600-605 on a bounce

key_levels.why: 7/15 reversal low and prior support; the floor under megacap tech
Breakout base of my open long; holding it keeps the bank trend intact
7/13 low and the base metals are trying to build
key_levels.trigger: a hold and reclaim of 372 on a retest
